label: Bitcoin
summary: UTXO-based proof-of-work settlement network with Bitcoin Core RPC APIs for node, wallet, mining, network, and transaction operations.

label: Ethereum
summary: Account-based smart contract network whose execution clients expose Ethereum JSON-RPC for apps and tooling.

label: Solana
summary: High-throughput smart contract chain with HTTP and WebSocket RPC interfaces and account-centric runtime semantics.

label: JSON-RPC
summary: Request-response interface pattern used by Ethereum execution clients and many crypto node APIs.

label: REST APIs
summary: HTTP resource APIs commonly used by hosted indexers, explorers, custody systems, and developer platforms.

label: eth_getBalance
summary: Ethereum JSON-RPC method that returns an account balance at a block tag.

label: eth_sendRawTransaction
summary: Ethereum JSON-RPC method used to broadcast a signed transaction to the network.

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast signed Ethereum transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Submit a hex-serialized signed transaction through Ethereum JSON-RPC.

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast signed Ethereum transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Post an eth_sendRawTransaction request from a backend worker.
multi_language_examples.code:
body := `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eth_sendRawTransaction","params":["` + signedTxHex + `"]}`
req, _ := http.NewRequest("POST", rpcURL, strings.NewReader(body))
req.Header.Set("content-type", "application/json")
resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
if err != nil { return err }
defer resp.Body.Close()
if resp.StatusCode >= 300 { return fmt.Errorf("rpc status %s", resp.Status) }

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast signed Ethereum transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Submit a signed transaction from an async Rust service.

label: sendrawtransaction
summary: Bitcoin Core RPC method used to submit a serialized signed transaction to the node.

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast Bitcoin raw transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Call Bitcoin Core RPC with a signed transaction hex.

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast Bitcoin raw transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Submit signed transaction hex to Bitcoin Core RPC.
multi_language_examples.code:
payload := fmt.Sprintf(`{"jsonrpc":"1.0","id":"ckg","method":"sendrawtransaction","params":["%s"]}`, signedTxHex)
req, _ := http.NewRequest("POST", rpcURL, strings.NewReader(payload))
req.SetBasicAuth(user, pass)
req.Header.Set("content-type", "text/plain")
resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
if err != nil { return err }
defer resp.Body.Close()

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast Bitcoin raw transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Use JSON-RPC over HTTP from a Rust broadcaster.

label: sendTransaction
summary: Solana RPC method used to submit a fully signed transaction.

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast Solana signed transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Serialize a signed transaction and submit base64 bytes through sendTransaction.

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast Solana signed transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Submit base64 transaction bytes through Solana JSON-RPC.
multi_language_examples.code:
encoded := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(signedTxBytes)
body := fmt.Sprintf(`{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"sendTransaction","params":["%s",{"encoding":"base64"}]}`, encoded)
resp, err := http.Post(rpcURL, "application/json", strings.NewReader(body))
if err != nil { return err }
defer resp.Body.Close()

multi_language_examples.title: Broadcast Solana signed transaction
multi_language_examples.summary: Send base64-encoded signed transaction bytes from Rust.

label: ERC-20
summary: Ethereum fungible token interface for balances, transfers, allowances, and approvals.

label: ERC-721
summary: Ethereum non-fungible token standard for unique token ownership and transfer semantics.

label: Build a wallet
summary: Create keys, derive addresses, query balances, construct and sign transactions, broadcast, and track confirmation.

label: Build a DeFi app
summary: Build smart contract and frontend flows around token standards, ABI calls, wallet connection, indexing, oracle inputs, and risk checks.

label: Build analytics/indexer
summary: Ingest chain data, normalize events, store queryable entities, and serve API/search surfaces for downstream apps.

label: Key generation
summary: Generate or derive private keys and seed material used to control accounts or UTXOs.

label: Address format
summary: Human-facing encoded destination or account identifier derived from public keys or account data.

label: Transaction creation
summary: Assemble chain-specific transaction payloads, fees, nonce or UTXO inputs, outputs, and execution metadata.

label: Transaction lifecycle
summary: Flow from transaction construction to signing, broadcast, mempool or leader processing, block inclusion, finality, and indexing.

label: Signed transaction
summary: Serialized transaction plus cryptographic authorization that can be broadcast to a network.

label: Signatures
summary: Cryptographic proof that a transaction or message was authorized by a private key holder.

label: ECDSA/secp256k1
summary: Signature scheme and elliptic curve used by Bitcoin and Ethereum transaction authorization.

label: Ed25519
summary: Edwards-curve signature system used by Solana accounts and transaction signatures.

label: Hashing
summary: One-way digest primitive used for transaction identifiers, addresses, Merkle roots, commitments, and proof systems.

label: Merkle trees
summary: Hash tree structure used to commit to sets of transactions or state and provide compact inclusion proofs.

label: Consensus
summary: Mechanism by which distributed validators or miners agree on valid chain history and ordering.

label: Proof of Work
summary: Consensus mechanism where miners expend computation to propose blocks and secure chain history.

label: Proof of Stake
summary: Consensus family where validators stake assets and can be rewarded or penalized for behavior.

label: Indexing
summary: Process of transforming raw chain data, logs, and transactions into queryable application-specific records.

label: ABI
summary: Application Binary Interface describing how to encode Ethereum smart contract calls and decode events.

label: Wallet app
summary: Application that manages keys, displays balances, sends transactions, and tracks confirmations across one or more chains.

label: DeFi app
summary: Application that interacts with smart contracts, token standards, wallets, oracles, and indexed events.

label: Replay risk
summary: Risk that a valid signed transaction or message can be reused in another context without proper domain separation.

label: Private key loss
summary: Irrecoverable loss or theft of signing authority caused by poor key storage, backup, or custody design.

label: Reorg risk
summary: Risk that recently included transactions are displaced by a competing chain history before sufficient finality.

label: Query ETH balance snippet
summary: Minimal fetch-based JSON-RPC example for calling eth_getBalance.
code: await fetch(rpcUrl, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ jsonrpc: '2.0', id: 1, method: 'eth_getBalance', params: [address, 'latest'] }) });

label: Cross-Chain State Verification
summary: Verify state, messages, or commitments from one chain inside another execution or settlement context.

label: BLS12-381
summary: Pairing-friendly elliptic curve used in proof systems and signature aggregation contexts.

label: Pairings
summary: Bilinear maps used by pairing-based signatures, commitments, and zk proof verification.

label: zk-SNARKs
summary: Succinct non-interactive proofs used to verify computation or state transitions with compact on-chain checks.

label: KZG commitments
summary: Polynomial commitment scheme used for succinct openings in data availability and proof systems.

label: Signature aggregation
summary: Combine many signatures or attestations into a compact verification artifact for consensus or coordination.

label: EVM Runtime
summary: Ethereum Virtual Machine execution environment for smart contracts and ABI-encoded calls.

label: SVM Runtime
summary: Solana Virtual Machine execution model for parallel account-based transaction processing.

label: WASM Runtime
summary: WebAssembly execution runtime used by several smart contract and app-chain ecosystems.

label: Payload signing
summary: Prepare canonical bytes for a transaction, message, or extrinsic and authorize them using a wallet, KMS, HSM, or policy-controlled signer.

label: Turnkey-style KMS signing
summary: Policy-controlled secure signing pattern for authorizing blockchain payloads without exposing private keys to application servers.
implementation_notes: Generate canonical payload bytes before signing.
Attach chain id, domain, genesis hash, or block hash to prevent replay.
Keep policy, quorum, and audit rules outside application request handlers.

label: Substrate raw extrinsic template
summary: SCALE-encoded Substrate extrinsic payload shape with call, era, nonce, tip, runtime versions, genesis hash, and block hash.

label: ERC-20 transfer calldata template
summary: ABI-encoded ERC-20 transfer call template for constructing token transfer transaction data.

label: Merkle proof verification pattern
summary: Verify a leaf against a Merkle root by hashing sibling nodes in order and comparing the computed root to the trusted commitment.

label: Remix EVM sandbox
summary: Browser-based EVM development environment for testing Solidity contracts, calldata, and transaction flows.

label: Replay domain guardrail
summary: Bind signatures to explicit chain, domain, genesis hash, nonce, or recent block context to prevent replay across contexts.

label: Build an offline transaction signer
summary: Construct canonical transaction bytes offline, delegate signing to wallet or KMS infrastructure, normalize signature output, and broadcast the serialized transaction.

multi_language_examples.title: Offline signing pipeline
multi_language_examples.summary: Construct canonical bytes, hash them, delegate signing, and assemble the signed transaction.

multi_language_examples.title: Offline signing pipeline
multi_language_examples.summary: Backend-oriented signer orchestration with explicit preimage and digest boundaries.

multi_language_examples.title: Offline signing pipeline
multi_language_examples.summary: Rust flow for deterministic preimage construction and signer delegation.

label: Ethereum EIP-155 signing preimage template
summary: Legacy Ethereum signing payload shape that includes chain id to prevent cross-chain replay before hashing and ECDSA signing.

label: AWS KMS secp256k1 signing pattern
summary: Use an AWS KMS asymmetric secp256k1 key to sign a chain-specific digest while the private key remains inside KMS.
implementation_notes: Use secp256k1 key spec for Ethereum-style ECDSA.
Pass DIGEST only after computing the chain-specific transaction digest.
KMS returns DER-encoded ECDSA signatures, not Ethereum-ready r/s/v.

multi_language_examples.title: AWS KMS secp256k1 Sign
multi_language_examples.summary: Sign a precomputed Ethereum-style digest with AWS KMS.

multi_language_examples.title: AWS KMS secp256k1 Sign
multi_language_examples.summary: Use the AWS SDK for Go to sign a digest and decode DER ECDSA.

multi_language_examples.title: AWS KMS secp256k1 Sign
multi_language_examples.summary: Call KMS Sign from Rust and normalize DER output.

label: ECDSA DER to r/s/v normalization snippet
summary: Decode ASN.1 DER ECDSA output into fixed-width r and s values, then derive recovery id v for Ethereum-style serialized transactions.

label: Turnkey raw payload signing pattern
summary: Use Turnkey sign_raw_payload to sign explicitly encoded payload bytes with a selected hash function and policy-controlled signer.
implementation_notes: Choose NO_OP only when the payload is already the exact digest or signing bytes expected by the chain.
Record the hash function and encoding with the transaction audit trail.
Map returned r/s/v or signature bytes into the chain-specific serialization format.

multi_language_examples.title: Turnkey raw payload signing
multi_language_examples.summary: Submit exact digest bytes to Turnkey with explicit encoding and hash mode.

multi_language_examples.title: Turnkey raw payload signing
multi_language_examples.summary: Post a sign_raw_payload activity from a Go backend.

multi_language_examples.title: Turnkey raw payload signing
multi_language_examples.summary: Build an explicit Turnkey raw-payload request from Rust.

label: Ed25519 KMS mode guardrail
summary: Ed25519 signing APIs distinguish raw-message signing from prehashed digest signing; mixing these modes can produce invalid or unsafe signatures.
implementation_notes: Confirm whether the target chain expects raw message signing or prehashed signing.
Do not send a digest as RAW unless the chain explicitly signs digest bytes as the message.
Persist MessageType and algorithm in audit logs.

label: Signing byte boundary guardrail
summary: Every offline signer must explicitly define whether the signer receives structured transaction fields, serialized transaction bytes, or a final digest.
implementation_notes: Separate transaction construction from signing authorization.
Log payload encoding, hash function, chain id, nonce, and expected public key.
Reject ambiguous payloads that omit domain or chain context.

label: Deterministic serialization
summary: Canonical byte encoding of protocol data before hashing, signing, storage, or on-chain execution.

label: RLP encoding
summary: Ethereum Recursive Length Prefix encoding for byte arrays and nested lists, used by legacy transaction serialization.

label: SCALE codec
summary: Substrate compact binary codec whose correctness depends on runtime metadata, type order, compact integer rules, and signed extension layout.

label: DAG-CBOR
summary: IPLD-compatible deterministic CBOR encoding used in Filecoin message and content-addressed data flows.

label: Ethereum legacy RLP byte template
summary: Byte-level checklist for producing an EIP-155 legacy Ethereum signing digest and signed transaction envelope.
implementation_notes: Do not sign the signed envelope.
Do not omit chainId, 0, 0 from the EIP-155 preimage.
Normalize field types before RLP: integers as minimal big-endian bytes and empty values as empty byte arrays.

label: Substrate SCALE signing byte template
summary: Byte-level checklist for constructing Substrate extrinsic signing payloads using SCALE and runtime metadata.
implementation_notes: Refresh metadata after runtime upgrades.
Encode call indexes from metadata, not hard-coded guesses.
Signed extensions and additional signed fields must match the target chain runtime.

label: Filecoin CBOR message byte template
summary: Byte-level checklist for canonical Filecoin message serialization before CID computation and signing.
implementation_notes: Do not sign JSON or UI-rendered message text.
Preserve canonical field order and binary address/value encodings.
Recompute CID from exact CBOR bytes used for signing.

label: Bad signature diagnostic
summary: Debug path for invalid transaction signatures caused by mismatched serialization, hashing, domain separation, or signer output normalization.
code:
1. Reconstruct the unsigned signing payload from chain metadata.
2. Hex-dump every field before serialization.
3. Compare serialized preimage against a known SDK output.
4. Verify hash function and KMS MessageType/encoding.
5. Decode signature output and check r/s/v or signature byte format.
6. Recompute sender/public key from signature before broadcasting.
implementation_notes: Most Bad Signature errors are preimage mismatches, not broken cryptography.
Always compare against a trusted SDK encoder before blaming KMS.
Persist hex preimages and hashes in non-secret audit logs.

label: Staking & nominations
summary: Validator selection, stake bonding, nominations or delegations, rewards, slashing, and activation/deactivation timing.

label: Polkadot staking & nominations
summary: Live Polkadot staking context: validator set, nominations, bonding duration, minimum active bond, eras, rewards, and slashing records.

label: Avalanche P-Chain staking
summary: Live Avalanche P-Chain staking context: current validators, pending validators, staking constraints, delegation, and reward-related parameters.

label: Polkadot.js live state provider
summary: Runtime-aware API provider for Polkadot SDK chains that can read constants, storage state, transactions, and metadata-generated endpoints.

label: Avalanche P-Chain RPC provider
summary: JSON-RPC provider for Avalanche P-Chain validator, staking, subnet, balance, reward, and transaction methods.

label: Staking parameter staleness guardrail
summary: Reject staking decisions when validator counts, bonding thresholds, slashing windows, or warm-up/cool-down periods come from stale snapshots.
implementation_notes: Display snapshot age beside every staking value.
Use runtime constants for constants and storage queries for state.
Block transaction construction if required network parameters are stale or missing.
